name: salesforcetesting2-l1-ticket-corpus
description: Golden L1 Sales/Service Cloud tickets for auto-resolution eval against salesforcetesting2.

tickets.subject: Lead conversion error: Missing pricebook entry on automatic line item mapping
tickets.issueText:
Lead conversion error: Missing pricebook entry on automatic line item mapping

I am trying to convert a qualified lead. Our system is configured to auto-create an opportunity and map standard product lines from the lead custom fields. However, when I click 'Convert', I get an error: 'System.DmlException: Insert failed. First exception on row 0; Standard Price Book has no price defined for product B2B-Enterprise'. I can't proceed with creating the opportunity. How do I fix this?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: This occurs because the product selected on the lead does not have an active price entry in the Standard Pricebook. 1. Go to the Products tab and find product 'B2B-Enterprise'. 2. Check the Pricebooks related list. 3. Click 'Add to Price Book', select the Standard Pricebook, enter a list price, and check 'Active'. 4. Return to the lead and re-run the conversion process.

tickets.subject: How to unlock an Opportunity currently stuck in a multi-stage approval process
tickets.issueText:
How to unlock an Opportunity currently stuck in a multi-stage approval process

An opportunity I own is locked because it was submitted for a commercial discount approval. The regional manager who needs to approve it is out of office for two weeks. I need to modify the product quantities immediately because the client changed their mind. There is no 'Recall' button visible on my page layout. How can I unlock this record?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: As an L1 agent, verify the approval process rules. Users cannot recall if the process locks it strictly for admins/assignees. 1. Navigate to Setup > Approval Processes > Process Visualizer to confirm the current actor. 2. Since the user doesn't see 'Recall', use system administrator privileges (or delegated approver permissions) to click the 'Recall Approval Request' button at the top of the Opportunity record on behalf of the user, or reassign the pending step to the manager's designated backup.

tickets.subject: Quote Syncing error: 'Fields do not match' when syncing Quote Line Items to Opportunity
tickets.issueText:
Quote Syncing error: 'Fields do not match' when syncing Quote Line Items to Opportunity

I am trying to sync an active Quote back to its parent Opportunity so the forecasting data updates. When I click 'Start Sync', it errors out saying: 'Quote syncing failed because the field Discount__c on Quote Line Item does not match the data type or configuration of the Opportunity Product field.' Both fields exist. Please tell me how to resolve this sync conflict.
tickets.expectedL1Answer: Quote syncing requires exact matching data types, lengths, and API names for custom fields mapped between Quote Line Items and Opportunity Products. 1. Go to Setup > Object Manager > Quote Line Item and check 'Discount__c' properties (e.g., it is Percent, 18, 2). 2. Go to Opportunity Product and check its custom 'Discount__c' field. 3. If there is a precision mismatch (e.g., Number instead of Percent, or different decimal places), notify L2/Admin to align the metadata. If it's a simple value formatting issue, clear the field value on the quote, sync, and re-enter data.

tickets.subject: Cannot activate Order: Product bundle status must be in 'Available' state
tickets.issueText:
Cannot activate Order: Product bundle status must be in 'Available' state

I have successfully generated an Order from a synced Quote. Now I am trying to change the Status from 'Draft' to 'Activated' so the ERP integration picks it up. However, I get a validation error: 'Order Activation Failed: One or more line items contain products that are decommissioned or inactive.' I verified the primary products are active. What am I doing wrong?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: The primary product may be active, but one of its child components/options in a product bundle has been marked inactive. 1. Open the Order and look at the Order Products related list. 2. Click into each individual Product record linked there. 3. Identify which sub-component has the 'Active' checkbox unchecked. 4. If it was deactivated by mistake, reactivate it in the Product object. If it's intentionally decommissioned, remove that line item from the Order/Quote and substitute it before activating.

tickets.subject: How to generate a renewal Contract directly from a closed-won Opportunity
tickets.issueText:
How to generate a renewal Contract directly from a closed-won Opportunity

I just closed a major deal and set the Opportunity stage to 'Closed Won'. I selected 'Contract Required' on the account record, but the Contract record didn't auto-generate under the Account's Contract related list. Can you explain the exact steps I need to take to manually spin up the initial contract and link it to this closed deal?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: To generate a contract from an Opportunity standard lifecycle: 1. Ensure the Account record has an associated Contract or is enabled for generation. 2. On the Opportunity record, locate the standard 'Contract' lookup field. 3. If empty, go to the parent Account, click 'New' under the Contracts related list, fill in Contract Start Date, Contract Term (months), and save it. 4. Go back to the Opportunity, edit the 'Contract' lookup field to link this newly created Contract, and then check the 'Contracted' checkbox on the Opportunity to trigger automated asset/subscription generation if CPQ is used.

tickets.subject: Lead assigned to wrong sales queue despite explicit postal code rules
tickets.issueText:
Lead assigned to wrong sales queue despite explicit postal code rules

We have an automated rule that assigns leads with zip code 90210 to the West Coast Queue. I just created a lead with that zip code, but it bypassed assignment entirely and stayed assigned to me. How do I force the system to re-evaluate the assignment rules for this specific lead?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: When creating or editing a lead manually, the assignment rules do not run automatically unless a hidden checkbox is ticked. 1. Open the affected Lead record and click 'Edit'. 2. Scroll to the very bottom of the edit layout screen. 3. Check the box labeled 'Assign using active assignment rules'. 4. Click 'Save'. The system will re-process the zip code and correctly assign the lead to the West Coast Queue.

tickets.subject: Negative price error when adding a custom volume discount to an Order Line Item
tickets.issueText:
Negative price error when adding a custom volume discount to an Order Line Item

I am working on Order ORD-009432. The customer negotiated a unique bulk discount, so I am trying to manually adjust the unit price on an Order Product line to a negative value or apply a negative line item to show a credit rebate. Salesforce keeps blocking me with an error: 'Unit Price cannot be negative.' How can I apply a financial adjustment to this line item?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: Standard Salesforce Sales Cloud validation prevents the core 'Unit Price' field on Order Products from being negative. To properly document a volume credit or rebate: 1. Do not use a negative unit price on a standard line item. 2. Instead, use the 'Discount' percentage field on the Order Product to lower the value. 3. If a flat-rate deduction is mandatory, create a dedicated Product record named 'Bulk Rebate Credit' that allows a negative price (if allowed in Pricebook configurations) or handle it via an Order-level adjustment field.

tickets.subject: Missing 'Add Products' button on an Opportunity record layout
tickets.issueText:
Missing 'Add Products' button on an Opportunity record layout

I need to build out a pipeline projection for a client, but when I navigate to the Products related list on my newly created Opportunity, the 'Add Products' and 'Choose Price Book' buttons are entirely missing. My colleague can see them on her opportunities. What steps do I need to follow to make them appear?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: The 'Add Products' button disappears under two conditions: either a Pricebook hasn't been selected yet, or the Opportunity is locked/associated with a synced Quote. 1. Look for a 'Price Book' field in the Products related list header; if blank, click the dropdown arrow and select 'Choose Price Book'. 2. If a Pricebook is already selected but buttons are missing, check if there is an active synced Quote. When a Quote is syncing, product management must happen exclusively on the Quote Line Items, which then automatically push to the Opportunity.

tickets.subject: Lead Conversion duplicates: How to merge lead into an existing Contact instead of creating a new one
tickets.issueText:
Lead Conversion duplicates: How to merge lead into an existing Contact instead of creating a new one

I am converting a lead for 'Jane Miller' from 'TechCorp'. TechCorp already exists in our system, and Jane Miller is already listed as a contact there from an old deal. When I go through the conversion screen, how do I prevent Salesforce from creating a second, duplicate Jane Miller contact record?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: During lead conversion, Salesforce provides options to avoid duplication. 1. Click 'Convert' on the Lead record. 2. In the 'Account' section, select 'Choose Existing' and search for TechCorp. 3. In the 'Contact' section, look closely at the radio buttons. Salesforce will auto-search for matching names; select 'Choose Existing Contact' and select the existing Jane Miller record. 4. Complete conversion. This appends the lead's historical data and activities directly onto the pre-existing contact record.

tickets.subject: Collaborative Forecasting not showing newly closed-won pipeline revenue
tickets.issueText:
Collaborative Forecasting not showing newly closed-won pipeline revenue

I closed a $50k deal yesterday and marked it 'Closed Won'. However, when my sales manager pulls up the Collaborative Forecasts hierarchy view for Q3, my closed-won attainment bar hasn't increased at all. It still shows my previous totals. Why is this revenue missing from the forecast dashboard?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: This is typically caused by a delayed forecast roll-up cache or an incorrect Forecast Category assignment on the opportunity stage. 1. Open the Opportunity and check the 'Forecast Category' field (which is driven by Stage). Ensure it is set to 'Closed' (not omitted). 2. Verify that the Close Date is within the correct fiscal quarter limits (Q3). 3. Note that Collaborative Forecasts can take up to 15-30 minutes to asynchronously refresh across complex organizational hierarchies. Advise user to check back shortly.

tickets.subject: PDF Generation fails on Quote: Image asset broken or missing access rights
tickets.issueText:
PDF Generation fails on Quote: Image asset broken or missing access rights

When I try to use the standard 'Create Quote PDF' feature on my quote record, the system generates a document but it completely leaves out our corporate logo, displaying a broken image icon instead. The rest of the table looks fine. How do I fix the template layout so our logo loads correctly for customers?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: This happens when the corporate logo image stored in Salesforce Documents or Files is not marked for external visibility or has been deleted. 1. Go to Files/Documents and locate the logo file. 2. Ensure the file permissions are set to 'Publicly Available Image' or shared globally. 3. Copy the clean Document ID or Content Document URL. 4. Navigate to Setup > Quote Templates, edit the active layout, select the Image component, replace the broken asset reference path with the updated public URL, and save.

tickets.subject: How to split a single Opportunity into multiple distinct Orders for separate distribution sites
tickets.issueText:
How to split a single Opportunity into multiple distinct Orders for separate distribution sites

I have an opportunity with 10 different line items. The client wants 5 items shipped to their Chicago branch next month, and the other 5 items shipped to Miami in November. Since they require separate delivery tracking, how do I break down this single opportunity into two separate Salesforce Orders?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: Salesforce allows creating multiple Orders from a single parent record. 1. Navigate to the Orders related list on the Opportunity and click 'New Order'. 2. Create the first Order for the Chicago site, setting the appropriate delivery date, and save. 3. Inside that Order, click 'Add Products', select the specific 5 items meant for Chicago, and input their quantities. 4. Go back to the Opportunity, click 'New Order' again, repeat for Miami, and select the remaining products. This maps multiple custom orders back to a unified pipeline transaction.

tickets.subject: Cannot edit Pricebook field on Opportunity after initial selection
tickets.issueText:
Cannot edit Pricebook field on Opportunity after initial selection

I accidentally selected the 'EMEA Enterprise Pricebook' for an Opportunity when I should have selected 'APAC Retail Pricebook'. Now, when I try to change it, the Pricebook field is greyed out and uneditable. How can I clear out this selection and pick the correct currency catalog?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: Salesforce prevents changing an Opportunity's Pricebook if there are already products added under the current selection. To switch it: 1. Go to the Products related list on the Opportunity. 2. Click the action dropdown next to each product and select 'Delete' until the products list is completely empty. 3. Once all line items are deleted, return to the detail page or products header. The 'Choose Price Book' button or field will be unlocked and editable again.

tickets.subject: Shared Activities configuration: How to log a meeting against multiple Contacts simultaneously
tickets.issueText:
Shared Activities configuration: How to log a meeting against multiple Contacts simultaneously

I held a discovery call with a prospect company where 4 different contacts from their executive team attended. Right now, I can only seem to log the completed Event task against one contact lookup. How do I track this single meeting against all 4 contact timelines without manually rewriting the note 4 times?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: This requires utilizing Salesforce's 'Shared Activities' feature (Allow Users to Relate Multiple Contacts to Tasks and Events). 1. Open the New Event/Task window. 2. In the 'Name' (WhoId) lookup field, select the first contact. 3. Notice the small '+ Add' or multi-select icon next to the search field. Click it. 4. You can now add up to 50 individual contact records to this single activity. When saved, the activity will replicate across all 4 individual contact history timelines while rolling up neatly to the parent Account.

tickets.subject: Contract Status locked: Cannot transition Contract from Draft directly to Activated
tickets.issueText:
Contract Status locked: Cannot transition Contract from Draft directly to Activated

I am an account manager trying to finalize a contract (CTR-9982). The client signed the hard copy, so I am changing the status from 'Draft' to 'Activated'. When I click save, I get a system message: 'A contract cannot be activated until it has been reviewed and approved by Legal.' There is no approval submit button. How do I process this?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: This indicates an organization-wide validation rule or a customized status path dependency matching your business governance rules. 1. Check if there is a 'Legal Approved' checkbox field on the Contract layout. 2. If it is unchecked, verify if a separate approval history process needs to be triggered by changing the stage to 'In Review' first. 3. Instruct the user to move the contract to 'In Review', which automates notification to the Legal queue. Once Legal ticks their checkbox, the status path unlocks to allow manual or automated Activation.

tickets.subject: Lead Duplication alert blocking valid cross-sell entry
tickets.issueText:
Lead Duplication alert blocking valid cross-sell entry

I am trying to log a new inbound lead for an existing contact who wants to purchase a totally separate product line. Every time I hit save, Salesforce blocks me entirely with an alert stating that a duplicate record exists and refuses to save it. How can I bypass this rule to capture this cross-sell interest?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: The system's Duplicate Rules are configured to 'Block' instead of 'Alert/Allow' on matching names or emails. 1. Review the matching record link provided in the warning text to confirm if it's the exact same individual. 2. Since it's a new separate sales interest, rather than creating a duplicate Lead, standard process dictates navigating directly to the pre-existing Account/Contact record and creating a brand new 'Opportunity' directly from their profile to track the cross-sell, bypassing unnecessary lead pollution.

tickets.subject: Opportunity split revenue allocations calculating incorrect percentages
tickets.issueText:
Opportunity split revenue allocations calculating incorrect percentages

I am co-selling an opportunity with a colleague. We set up an Opportunity Split where I should get 70% credit and he gets 30%. However, when we look at the splitting layout, it is throwing an error that the total must equal 100%, even though our math is correct. It seems to be counting a hidden 100% overlay split allocation somewhere. Help!
tickets.expectedL1Answer: Salesforce supports multiple split types (e.g., Revenue Splits which must equal 100%, and Overlay Splits which can exceed 100%). 1. Check which section you are editing. You are likely adding your colleague to the standard Revenue Split list without decreasing your own default allocation down from 100% first. 2. Edit your own allocation percentage to 70% first, then add the colleague row at 30%, ensuring the cumulative sum equals exactly 100.00%. Save changes.

tickets.subject: Pricing discrepancy: Total Quote price doesn't match cumulative Line Item amounts due to tax calculation
tickets.issueText:
Pricing discrepancy: Total Quote price doesn't match cumulative Line Item amounts due to tax calculation

On Quote QT-5561, the individual line items add up to exactly $10,000. However, the 'Grand Total' summary field at the bottom displays $10,850. There are no manual discounts or shipping fees applied in the fields. How is this extra $850 being appended, and how can I view or adjust the line item parameters?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: This indicates an active automated Tax Calculation layout or integration running on the Quote object. 1. Check the 'State/Postal Code' fields on the Quote's Billing Address. 2. If an address is entered, the system calls an internal calculation matrix or external handler (like Avalara/Vertex) to auto-append regional sales tax (e.g., 8.5%). 3. To modify or waive this tax, locate the 'Tax Exempt' checkbox or 'Tax Override' reason field on the Quote header, supply the appropriate credential, and click save to re-calculate.

tickets.subject: How to reduce an existing Order quantity (Creating a Reduction Order)
tickets.issueText:
How to reduce an existing Order quantity (Creating a Reduction Order)

A customer ordered 50 units of our product via ORD-00231, which is already 'Activated'. They just called to cut their order down to 40 units before shipping. Because the order is activated, I can't modify the quantity field directly. What is the standard procedure to reduce line items on an active order?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: To reduce quantities on activated records without violating tracking compliance, use Reduction Orders: 1. Navigate to the activated Order record. 2. Click the 'Reduce Order' button (or standard action dropdown). 3. Enter the Reduction Order details (Effective Date, etc.) and save. 4. In the Reduction Order Products related list, select the items being returned/reduced, and input the quantity to reduce (e.g., enter 10 to drop the balance from 50 down to 40). 5. Activate the Reduction Order to sync back to inventory.

tickets.subject: Mass updating Lead/Contact Member Statuses inside a marketing campaign
tickets.issueText:
Mass updating Lead/Contact Member Statuses inside a marketing campaign

I have a marketing Campaign with over 500 members currently in 'Sent' status. We just completed the webinar and I have a CSV list of the 150 people who actually attended. How do I mass update just those 150 specific members to 'Attended' status in Salesforce without clicking into each individual member profile?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: To mass update member statuses efficiently without data loader tools: 1. Open the Campaign record. 2. Click the 'Manage Campaign Members' dropdown button and select 'Import Leads and Contacts' or 'Data Import Wizard'. 3. Select 'Update Campaign Member Statuses'. 4. Upload your CSV containing the 150 emails/IDs. 5. Map the status field destination to 'Attended', match by Email or Salesforce ID, and run the wizard. This batch updates the status records safely within 2 minutes.

tickets.subject: How to attach a verified Knowledge Article to an open Case and email it as a PDF
tickets.issueText:
How to attach a verified Knowledge Article to an open Case and email it as a PDF

I am working on an intricate technical issue. I found an internal Knowledge Article that perfectly explains the resolution steps. How do I attach this article directly to the active Case record layout so it is tracked in our resolution metrics, and how can I send its text content straight to the customer's email box via the console?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: To link and share knowledge within the Service Console: 1. Locate the Knowledge Component sidebar on the right side of your Case layout screen. 2. Search for and open the relevant article. 3. Click the dropdown arrow next to the article title and select 'Insert Article into Email' or 'Attach Article'. 4. Attaching binds it to the Case's Articles related list. Inserting into email auto-copies the text content directly into your active Case Email Publisher window. Click send to share with the customer.

tickets.subject: Case Milestone timer not appearing despite valid customer support Contract
tickets.issueText:
Case Milestone timer not appearing despite valid customer support Contract

I just opened a high-priority incident for an enterprise account. They have an active service Contract, but the standard SLA Milestone Countdown Timer widget is completely missing from the console sidebar. This means our response time tracking is broken. How do I apply their contractual entitlement terms to this case record?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: Milestone timers are driven by the 'Entitlement' field on the Case layout. Even if an Account has a Contract, individual cases must explicitly link to an Entitlement record. 1. On the Case Detail page, locate the empty 'Entitlement Name' lookup field. 2. Click the search lens; it will filter and show active entitlements linked to that Account or Contract. 3. Select the appropriate SLA tier entitlement (e.g., 'Gold 24/7 Support'). 4. Click Save. The Milestone component will instantly appear and initiate the countdown based on the SLA terms.

tickets.subject: Knowledge Article translation draft stuck in draft queue, cannot publish
tickets.issueText:
Knowledge Article translation draft stuck in draft queue, cannot publish

I authored a technical troubleshooting manual in English and submitted it for translation into Spanish. Our regional team completed the Spanish translation draft, but when I try to hit 'Publish' on the Spanish version, the button is greyed out. My profile has knowledge manager rights. How do I release this version?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: Salesforce Knowledge isolates publishing rights per language channel and article status. 1. Check if the translated version is currently assigned to an explicit Translation Queue or validation step. 2. You must first navigate to the 'Knowledge Articles' tab and switch your list view to 'Draft Translations'. 3. Select the Spanish draft, click 'Submit for Publication' or 'Submit for Review' to pass it past the translation workflow block. If you aren't a designated member of the Spanish translation group, a user within that specific language queue must authorize the publication.

tickets.subject: Omni-Channel capacity issue: Agent not receiving new incoming support chats
tickets.issueText:
Omni-Channel capacity issue: Agent not receiving new incoming support chats

I am logged into the Service Console and my Omni-Channel status widget is set to 'Available - Chat/Email'. There are currently 12 customers waiting in the routing queue, but no work items are pushing to my screen. My current active case load is clear. How do I fix my configuration to start accepting chats?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: This is typically caused by 'ghost capacity exhaustion' where closed items are still occupying your active agent allocation limit. 1. Look inside your Omni-Channel component at the bottom of the console screen. 2. Click on the 'My Work' tab. 3. Check if there are old, unresolved tabs or chat sessions still listed as open. 4. Click the 'X' icon next to each completed work item inside the Omni widget to officially release your capacity allocation. Once your capacity falls below 100%, Omni-Channel will immediately push new cases to you.

tickets.subject: How to link a service Contract line item to a newly deployed asset tracking record
tickets.issueText:
How to link a service Contract line item to a newly deployed asset tracking record

We just shipped a specialized server to a client, and I created an 'Asset' record for it under their Account. They purchased a 3-year extended hardware warranty contract (CTR-0029) for this item. How do I link this specific physical Asset to their Service Contract line items so that support agents can check warranty coverage?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: To link an Asset to a Service Contract via Contract Line Items: 1. Open the target Service Contract record. 2. Locate the 'Contract Line Items' related list and click 'New' or 'Add Products'. 3. Select the warranty line package product. 4. In the creation wizard screen, locate the 'Asset' lookup field. 5. Search for and select the specific server asset serial number record you generated. 6. Input price and term details, then save. This establishes deep mapping so agents viewing the Asset can see it is covered by that active contract.

tickets.subject: Customer outbound emails bouncing with '554: Delivery error: Threading ID invalid'
tickets.issueText:
Customer outbound emails bouncing with '554: Delivery error: Threading ID invalid'

A customer is trying to reply to an email sent out from Case #00331245, but their messages are bouncing back to them with a mail delivery error pointing to a malformed threading token or headers. How do I recover the original thread link so we don't lose track of their communication?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: This occurs if email headers or the modern Salesforce Email Threading Token (which replaced old Ref IDs) get mangled by the customer's secure corporate email gateway filtering. 1. Instruct the customer to compose a clean, fresh email to your inbound support address (e.g., support@company.com). 2. Have them paste the Case Number '#00331245' cleanly in the Subject line. 3. When it reaches Salesforce, Email-to-Case logic will scan the text. If it generates a new case, an L1 agent can use the standard 'Merge Cases' action to fuse the new incoming record back into the original master case timeline.

tickets.subject: How to set up a recurring Parent-Child relationship structure for sub-task tracking
tickets.issueText:
How to set up a recurring Parent-Child relationship structure for sub-task tracking

I have an incoming system implementation case that requires actions from our IT department, Finance group, and Shipping warehouse. I want to keep the main case open as an umbrella record and create three separate tracking sub-tasks under it for those teams. What are the operational steps to establish this link?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: To create a structured sub-task framework using Parent-Child relationships: 1. Open the primary master Case record. 2. Navigate to the 'Child Cases' related list and click 'New'. 3. Fill out the specific parameters for the IT department sub-task. Note that the 'Parent Case' lookup field will auto-populate with the original case number. 4. Click 'Save & New' to repeat for Finance and Shipping. This builds a hierarchical tree layout allowing the primary owner to monitor individual sub-group progress.

tickets.subject: Knowledge Search results showing outdated archived article versions to console agents
tickets.issueText:
Knowledge Search results showing outdated archived article versions to console agents

When agents type 'VPN Setup' into the global console search box, the search results page prominently returns an article written in 2021 that was supposedly archived months ago. The new, active article is buried deep down the list. How can I remove this old asset from appearing in internal searches?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: Archived articles should not appear in active search layouts unless search filtering is misconfigured. 1. Go to the Knowledge Articles tab. 2. Filter by 'Archived Articles' and locate the 2021 manual. 3. Check its configuration settings. Ensure that the checkbox field 'IsVisibleInApp' (Visible to Internal App Users) or 'Visible in Knowledge Base' is completely unchecked for this archived version. 4. If it remains indexed, request an L2 admin to run a Knowledge Search Index rebuild or modify the Article Search Layout parameters to exclude archived statuses entirely.

tickets.subject: Entitlement process milestone error: 'Case field update failed due to locked status'
tickets.issueText:
Entitlement process milestone error: 'Case field update failed due to locked status'

I am trying to resolve a case before the 1-hour critical response milestone expires. I added a case comment and changed the status to 'Waiting on Customer'. When I hit save, I get an error: 'Validation error on Case: Cannot modify case status because the underlying Entitlement Milestone is locked by an open system transaction.' How do I override this?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: This happens when an automated milestone trigger rule is firing concurrently to mark a milestone as 'Compliant' or 'Violated' right as you are manually altering the case. 1. Refresh your browser window to clear the transaction cache. 2. Check the Milestone tracker component to see if the milestone status has already changed asynchronously. 3. If the record remains locked, edit the 'Status' field again without modifying other custom fields simultaneously. This allows the background entitlement script to execute its updates independently without field validation collisions.

tickets.subject: How to manually trigger an override on an automated Case Escalation Rule rule path
tickets.issueText:
How to manually trigger an override on an automated Case Escalation Rule rule path

We have an automated rule that escalates any case left open for more than 4 hours straight to Level 2. I have a specialized client case that I am actively working on with an external vendor, and I want to stop it from escalating away from my queue automatically. How do I pause the escalation rule timer?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: Automated Escalation Rules check specific criteria to calculate execution. To manually override or pause an impending escalation: 1. Open the Case detail page. 2. Look for a custom field checkbox typically named 'Exempt from Escalation', 'Do Not Escalate', or a similar marker configured by your admin. 3. Alternatively, modify the Case 'Priority' down or change the Case 'Status' to an excluded status path like 'On Hold' or 'Under Vendor Review'. Escalation rules evaluate criteria dynamically; altering these monitored fields pushes the case out of the automation queue criteria.

tickets.subject: Console split view panel keeps disappearing on page refresh
tickets.issueText:
Console split view panel keeps disappearing on page refresh

Every time I open my Service Console tab layout and click on a case list view, I prefer using the 'Split View' format. However, whenever I refresh my Chrome browser tab or log out and log back in, the console defaults back to 'Table View' and I have to manually click to toggle Split View back on. How do I pin this layout permanently?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: Salesforce Console applications remember your navigation preferences via browser session cookies and pinned layouts. 1. In the Service Console, look at the narrow left side of the navigation bar where the list view is shown. 2. Toggle it to 'Split View'. 3. Notice the small pin icon or the drop-down arrow next to the object name (e.g., Cases). Select 'Pin This List'. 4. Ensure your local browser settings are not configured to automatically clear cookies/local cache storage upon refresh, as Salesforce relies on local storage parameters to retain console workspace states.

tickets.subject: Restricting Knowledge Article visibility to specific high-tier customer profiles on Experience Cloud Portal
tickets.issueText:
Restricting Knowledge Article visibility to specific high-tier customer profiles on Experience Cloud Portal

We have composed an internal article containing highly confidential API access keys that should only be visible to customers holding our 'Premier Platinum Partner' tier accounts on our Experience Cloud portal. Right now, it's visible to all logged-in portal users. How do I change the visibility parameters?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: To control article visibility for external portals accurately, use Knowledge Data Categories and Channel Visibility. 1. Open the specific Knowledge Article draft. 2. On the right side, locate the 'Data Categories' module and click 'Edit'. 3. Map the article to a category restricted to Premier users (e.g., Support Tiers > Platinum). 4. Next, verify under the 'Properties' panel that 'Visible in Customer Portal' is checked, but ensure the Data Category Visibility group configurations managed by your system administrator limit that specific category exclusively to the Premier Portal User Profile.

tickets.subject: Case assignment rules failing to route cases created via manual 'Clone' action
tickets.issueText:
Case assignment rules failing to route cases created via manual 'Clone' action

When customers send emails, our routing rules work perfectly. But when I take an old resolved case and click the standard 'Clone' button to open a new related ticket for a customer, the new case stays assigned to me instead of automatically routing to the Tier 1 Queue. How do I force assignment rules to run during a clone?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: Like Lead objects, manual cloning bypasses routing engines unless specified. 1. When you click 'Clone', the case record edit layout opens before saving. 2. Look at the bottom left or right corner of the edit frame for a checkbox labeled 'Assign using active assignment rules'. 3. Ensure this checkbox is checked before clicking 'Save'. If this box is completely missing from your layout, notify your admin to add the 'Default Assignment Checkbox' metadata element to the Case Page Layout properties.

tickets.subject: Contract Expiration batch warnings sending automated alerts to inactive account owners
tickets.issueText:
Contract Expiration batch warnings sending automated alerts to inactive account owners

Our system is sending automated email alerts warning that maintenance contracts are expiring in 30 days. However, these notifications are bouncing or being sent to former employees who are no longer with our company because they are still listed as Account Owners. How do I mass reassign these alerts?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: The automated alert is driven by a workflow rule or flow referencing the 'Account Owner' or 'Contract Owner' field value. 1. To fix the immediate alert failure, find the affected Service Contract records. 2. Click 'Change Owner' and transfer ownership to an active manager or generic shared system queue. 3. To prevent future incidents, inform the L2/Admin group to run an audit on deactivated users and mass-transfer all historic open accounts and active contracts owned by inactive personnel over to active team representatives.

tickets.subject: Web-to-Case forms submitting spam tickets with blank email addresses
tickets.issueText:
Web-to-Case forms submitting spam tickets with blank email addresses

We are getting bombarded with 100+ spam tickets daily originating from our public website support contact form. The tickets contain weird strings in the description, and the customer email field is completely blank, which bypasses our standard verification rules. How can we configure Salesforce to reject these entries?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: Standard Web-to-Case captures any data pushed to its public URL endpoint and cannot enforce complex validation at the server level during entry. 1. Request your corporate webmaster to implement Google reCAPTCHA v3 directly on your website's HTML contact form. 2. Inside Salesforce, navigate to Setup > Web-to-Case, and ensure the 'Require reCAPTCHA Verification' option is toggled on. This blocks malicious bot submissions from hitting the Salesforce API wrapper without valid cryptographic tokens.

tickets.subject: How to edit a closed Case record to correct an entry typo without reopening the entire ticket
tickets.issueText:
How to edit a closed Case record to correct an entry typo without reopening the entire ticket

I resolved Case #00441928 and set the status to 'Closed'. I realized afterward that I made a glaring typographical error in a custom text field called 'Root Cause Summary'. Because the case is closed, the layout is locked down and the 'Edit' button is completely missing. How do I fix this typo?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: Salesforce locks page layouts entirely once a Case hits a status mapped to the 'Closed' status category. To make corrections without breaking metrics: 1. L1 agents generally do not have permission to edit closed records due to audit compliances. 2. Escalate this minor edit task to your L2 Support Lead or Admin. 3. The Administrator can use system tools, a special permission set ('Edit Closed Cases'), or temporarily toggle a validation rule to correct the text string directly within the record layout database without resetting the case lifecycle timers.

tickets.subject: Macro execution failing at step 3: 'Interaction Log component could not be found'
tickets.issueText:
Macro execution failing at step 3: 'Interaction Log component could not be found'

I am trying to run an efficiency macro named 'Standard Tech Closure' on my open console workspace. The macro successfully updates the status field and updates the contact, but then it abruptly stops with a bright red warning stating that the Interaction Log layout component cannot be located. How can I fix this macro tool?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: This happens when an agent runs a macro built for an older console layout specification or if the Feed-Based layout has been altered. 1. Check if you are currently viewing the Case via the 'Service Console' app or the standard 'Service' lightning app. Macros built for Console require utility bars. 2. Switch back to the official Service Console application. 3. Ensure the 'Log a Call' or 'Interaction Log' panel is minimized at the bottom of your workspace window. If it still fails, the macro metadata must be updated by L2 to target the newer Lightning Quick Action publisher instead of the legacy panel.

tickets.subject: How to mass-archive multiple obsolete Knowledge Articles at the same time
tickets.issueText:
How to mass-archive multiple obsolete Knowledge Articles at the same time

Our product development team just deprecated a legacy software product version. There are currently 35 active, published Knowledge Articles relating to that software version that are now totally obsolete. Is there a way to select all 35 records and archive them in bulk, or do I have to open each article individually?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: You can mass archive articles directly via the Articles tab layout view: 1. Navigate to the Knowledge Articles tab (or Knowledge app view). 2. Choose the 'Published Articles' list view. 3. Use the filter panel to isolate the articles related to the legacy product. 4. Check the select-all box on the left-hand column header, or manually check the 35 target rows. 5. Once selected, look at the action buttons at the top of the list view grid and click 'Archive'. 6. Confirm the execution date and click submit to process the entire list in one operation.

tickets.subject: Entitlement lookup field on Case showing unrelated data from separate accounts
tickets.issueText:
Entitlement lookup field on Case showing unrelated data from separate accounts

I am working on a case for 'Acme Corp'. When I click the magnifying glass lookup on the 'Entitlement Name' field, the lookup window displays service entitlements belonging to 'Globex Industries' and 'Initech'. This is highly confusing. How do I configure the lookup window to only show entitlements relevant to the case account?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: This indicates that lookup filter criteria have been cleared or are missing on the Case object definition. 1. This requires an adjustment by an Administrator, but as an L1 agent, you can bypass this immediately by manually typing the exact parent Account name into the search bar within the lookup popup menu. 2. Log a minor ticket enhancement request for L2/Admin to re-enable 'Lookup Filters' on the Case.Entitlement field, setting the criteria constraint strictly to: 'Current Case.Account ID equals Entitlement.Account ID', which instantly solves cross-account data leaking.

tickets.subject: Case ownership automatically reverting to default queue immediately after manual user reassignment
tickets.issueText:
Case ownership automatically reverting to default queue immediately after manual user reassignment

I am trying to hand off Case #00543211 to a field technician named Robert. I click 'Change Owner', select Robert's user profile name, and click save. The record updates, but as soon as I refresh the page layout, the owner automatically flips back to the 'Default Triage Queue' on its own. Why is the system overriding my manual reassignment?
tickets.expectedL1Answer: This is a classic symptom of an active Salesforce Case Assignment Rule running in the background during updates. 1. Look at the Case layout. Check if a flag or criteria parameter (like Country or Product Area) is still matching the default routing rules. 2. If an active assignment rule is configured to execute on every single edit, it will continuously re-evaluate and strip manual ownership away. 3. To bypass this, clear out or modify the trigger values causing the routing match, or uncheck the assignment rule execution box hidden at the bottom layout grid before saving your owner swap.
